input_1.summary: Input layer for RGB ImageNet images.
input_1.detailed: Accepts 3-channel (Red, Green, Blue) images of size 224x224. Normalization was applied across color channels to stabilize training gradients.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Standard RGB input dimensions allow features like textures and color borders to be learned.

conv2d_1.summary: Extracts coarse edge and color representations.
conv2d_1.detailed: Uses large 11x11 kernels with a stride of 4. This downsamples the input image resolution rapidly while finding initial shape primitives.
conv2d_1.keyTakeaway: Large early kernels reduce spatial size quickly but can miss fine details.

max_pool_1.detailed: Max pooling extracts the peak activation value over overlapping 3x3 windows with a stride of 2. Helps reduce spatial sensitivity.
max_pool_1.keyTakeaway: Overlapping pooling reduces spatial sensitivity and helps prevent overfitting.

conv2d_2.detailed: Applies 5x5 kernels. In the original paper, this computation was split across 2 GPUs to fit inside hardware constraints.
conv2d_2.keyTakeaway: Multi-GPU splitting was historically necessary due to small GPU memory buffers.

max_pool_2.detailed: Extracts peak activations over 3x3 grids, halving the spatial height/width to 13.
max_pool_2.keyTakeaway: Overlapping pooling maintains gradient flow during training.

conv2d_3.summary: First of three stacked convolutional layers.
conv2d_3.detailed: Uses 3x3 filters. Convolutions are stacked directly back-to-back without intermediate pooling, increasing depth.
conv2d_3.keyTakeaway: Back-to-back convolutions build non-linear capacity without losing spatial resolution.

conv2d_4.detailed: Keeps spatial size at 13x13. Helps the network build complex relationships between multi-channel features.
conv2d_4.keyTakeaway: Uniform channel widths maintain rich structural representations.

conv2d_5.detailed: Compresses channels from 384 back to 256 before entering the final pooling downsample.
conv2d_5.keyTakeaway: Slight channel shrinkage prepares features for fully connected mapping.

max_pool_3.detailed: Reduces spatial height/width to 6, resulting in a small 6x6 grid with 256 feature channels.
max_pool_3.keyTakeaway: Final pooling compresses high-level features into a standard spatial footprint.

flatten_1.detailed: Converts the spatial block into a single flat vector of length 9216, connecting it to the classification layers.
flatten_1.keyTakeaway: Bridges multi-channel grid filters and classification neurons.

dense_1.detailed: Uses 4096 units, accounting for over 60% of the model's total parameters. Dropout of 0.5 was applied here to prevent overfitting.
dense_1.keyTakeaway: Massive fully connected layers are prone to overfitting without dropout.

dense_2.detailed: Reduces complexity and filters representational features. Dropout of 0.5 was applied to regularize training.
dense_2.keyTakeaway: Successive dense layers consolidate model decisions before final classification.

output.detailed: Outputs class confidence scores. Softmax guarantees all 1000 probabilities sum to 1.0.
output.keyTakeaway: Softmax converts raw outputs into interpretable confidence percentages.

input_1.summary: RGB image inputs for the ConvNeXt network.
input_1.detailed: Accepts 224x224 pixels RGB color images. ConvNeXt starts downsampling immediately using a patchify-style convolution block rather than ResNet's heavy 7x7 conv stride-2 + maxpooling.
input_1.keyTakeaway: ConvNeXt processes raw RGB inputs using a modern patch projection.

stem_conv.summary: Downsamples image to 56x56 like a patch partition.
stem_conv.detailed: Uses a 4x4 convolution with stride 4. This mimics ViT's patch projection, setting a starting channel resolution of 96.
stem_conv.keyTakeaway: Replaces ResNet's 7x7 stride-2 conv and maxpool stack, yielding a cleaner starting feature map.

stem_ln.summary: Applies Layer Normalization after the stem.
stem_ln.detailed: Normalizes activations across the channel dimension (96). Replaces ResNet's Batch Normalization choice.
stem_ln.keyTakeaway: ConvNeXt uses LayerNorm throughout the model to align with Transformer training configurations.

stage1_blocks.summary: Modern blocks with large kernels and GELU.
stage1_blocks.detailed: Applies 7x7 depthwise convolutions (spatial mixing), followed by LayerNorm, 1x1 linear expansion to 384 channels, GELU activation, and 1x1 compression to 96 channels.
stage1_blocks.keyTakeaway: 7x7 kernels increase the receptive field early in the network, mimicking self-attention's window scope.

downsample1_conv.detailed: Uses a 2x2 conv with stride 2, normalized by an associated LayerNorm. Mimics ViT/Swin patch merging steps.
downsample1_conv.keyTakeaway: Replaces ResNet's convolution downsample blocks (which use stride-2 1x1 convs in residuals).

stage2_blocks.detailed: Applies ConvNeXt blocks at 28x28 resolution. Uses inverted bottleneck channels: 192 -> 768 -> 192.
stage2_blocks.keyTakeaway: Bottleneck channels are expanded inside the block (like MobileNetV2 inverted residuals) to improve representation capacity.

downsample2_conv.detailed: Reduces spatial height/width to 14x14. Prepares representations for the deep Stage 3 block.
downsample2_conv.keyTakeaway: Constructs multi-scale representations by decreasing spatial resolution.

stage3_blocks.detailed: The deepest stage of the network. Large 7x7 depthwise convolutions extract deep hierarchical structures, while inverted bottlenecks combine features across channels.
stage3_blocks.keyTakeaway: Corresponds to ResNet's deep Conv4 stage, accounting for ~47% of the model parameters.

downsample3_conv.summary: Reduces map size to final 7x7 resolution.
downsample3_conv.detailed: Final spatial downsampling step. Prepares ConvNeXt for Stage 4 class-specific extraction.

stage4_blocks.detailed: Applies the final 3 ConvNeXt blocks. Extracts complex semantic representations at 7x7 spatial size.
stage4_blocks.keyTakeaway: Extracts semantic visual attributes before global classification pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to 1D vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages features across the 7x7 spatial locations, generating a single 768-dimensional visual embedding vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Collapses spatial dimensions to prepare features for category logit mapping.

norm_out.detailed: Applies a final layer normalization to the collapsed feature vector, stabilizing outputs before categorical evaluation.

head.keyTakeaway: Maps features to ImageNet class logits.

output.keyTakeaway: Yields output confidence distribution over categories.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. Spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in downstream dense layers.
input_1.keyTakeaway: All input images are resized to 224x224x3 before forward propagation.

zero_padding2d.detailed: Adds 3 rows/columns of zeros to borders, preparing for the large 7x7 stem convolution.
zero_padding2d.keyTakeaway: Ensures stem output matches Keras shape exactly.

conv1/conv.summary: Extracts basic visual patterns and downsamples spatial size.
conv1/conv.detailed: Uses large 7x7 filters to capture initial visual contexts while downsampling resolution to 112x112.
conv1/conv.keyTakeaway: Stem convolutions reduce resolution for the dense blocks.

conv2_block1_0_bn.keyTakeaway: Applies Batch Normalization first (Pre-activation ResNet style).

conv2_block1_0_relu.keyTakeaway: Pre-activation layout ensures smoother gradient flow.

conv2_block1_1_conv.detailed: Limits the incoming channel count (64) down to a manageable size of 128 channels, keeping the parameter overhead of the subsequent 3x3 conv stable.
conv2_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: Bottlenecks limit parameter growth in densely connected blocks.

conv2_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Applies BN prior to spatial conv.

conv2_block1_2_conv.detailed: The growth rate (k) is 32. This layer only needs to output 32 channels, which represent the new information added by this layer.
conv2_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes to the network state.

conv2_block1_concat.detailed: Concatenates the block's input (64 channels) with the new 32 channels produced by the 3x3 conv, outputting 96 channels.
conv2_block1_concat.analogy: Like building a sandwich where each layer adds a new slice of cheese, keeping all previous slices fully intact.
conv2_block1_concat.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.

pool4_conv.detailed: Compresses channel volume by 50% to prevent channel build-up across dense stages, managing memory constraints.
pool4_conv.keyTakeaway: Compression prevents the channel count from blowing up.

pool4_pool.summary: Reduces spatial size using average pooling.
pool4_pool.detailed: Average pooling calculates local feature averages. Downsamples spatial grids by 50%.
pool4_pool.keyTakeaway: Average pooling maps features to lower grid coordinates.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling averaging spatial channels.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 grid into a single feature vector of 1024 elements.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. Spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in downstream dense layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions.
input_1.keyTakeaway: All input images are resized to 224x224x3 before forward propagation.

zero_padding2d.detailed: Adds 3 rows/columns of zeros to borders, preparing for the large 7x7 stem convolution.
zero_padding2d.whyItMatters: Maintains spatial representation shapes in outer boundary elements.
zero_padding2d.keyTakeaway: Ensures stem output matches Keras shape exactly.

conv1/conv.summary: Extracts basic visual patterns and downsamples spatial size.
conv1/conv.detailed: Uses large 7x7 filters to capture initial visual contexts while downsampling resolution to 112x112.
conv1/conv.whyItMatters: Captures major shapes and boundaries while reducing computational density.
conv1/conv.keyTakeaway: Stem convolutions reduce resolution for the dense blocks.

conv1/bn.detailed: Balances data scaling across the stem network.
conv1/bn.whyItMatters: Avoids covariate shift early in training.

zero_padding2d_1.detailed: Pads input borders to maintain visual maps across max pooling.

pool1.detailed: Reduces features to a smaller grid size of 56x56.
pool1.whyItMatters: Saves computing cost for downstream dense block processing.

conv2_block1_0_bn.detailed: Ensures the concatenated representation is normalized before bottleneck projection.
conv2_block1_0_bn.whyItMatters: Keeps activations scaled properly across channels.
conv2_block1_0_bn.keyTakeaway: Applies Batch Normalization first (Pre-activation ResNet style).

conv2_block1_0_relu.whyItMatters: Saves gradients from vanishing in deep stacks.

conv2_block1_1_conv.detailed: Limits the incoming channel count (64) down to a size of 128 channels, keeping computation manageable.

conv2_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: Bottlenecks limit parameter growth in densely connected blocks.

conv2_block1_1_bn.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across bottleneck channels.
conv2_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Applies BN prior to spatial conv.

conv2_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes.

conv2_block1_concat.whyItMatters: Preserves features directly across all subsequent block stages.
conv2_block1_concat.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.

bn.detailed: Pre-activation setup requires a final BN layer before pooling.
bn.whyItMatters: Ensures balanced scaling of final outputs.

relu.whyItMatters: Prepares activated representation for global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling averaging spatial channels.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 grid into a single feature vector of 1664 elements.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. Spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in downstream dense layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions.
input_1.keyTakeaway: All input images are resized to 224x224x3 before forward propagation.

zero_padding2d.detailed: Adds 3 rows/columns of zeros to borders, preparing for the large 7x7 stem convolution.
zero_padding2d.whyItMatters: Maintains spatial representation shapes in outer boundary elements.
zero_padding2d.keyTakeaway: Ensures stem output matches Keras shape exactly.

conv1/conv.summary: Extracts basic visual patterns and downsamples spatial size.
conv1/conv.detailed: Uses large 7x7 filters to capture initial visual contexts while downsampling resolution to 112x112.
conv1/conv.whyItMatters: Captures major shapes and boundaries while reducing computational density.
conv1/conv.keyTakeaway: Stem convolutions reduce resolution for the dense blocks.

conv1/bn.detailed: Balances data scaling across the stem network.
conv1/bn.whyItMatters: Avoids covariate shift early in training.

zero_padding2d_1.detailed: Pads input borders to maintain visual maps across max pooling.

pool1.detailed: Reduces features to a smaller grid size of 56x56.
pool1.whyItMatters: Saves computing cost for downstream dense block processing.

pool2_pool.keyTakeaway: Average pooling maps features to lower grid coordinates.

conv3_block12_1_conv.keyTakeaway: Bottlenecks limit parameter growth in densely connected blocks.

conv3_block12_1_bn.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across bottleneck channels.

conv3_block12_2_conv.detailed: The growth rate (k) is 32. This layer only needs to output 32 channels, representing the new features discovered.

conv3_block12_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes.

conv3_block12_concat.detailed: Concatenates the block's input (480 channels) with the new 32 channels produced by the 3x3 conv, outputting 512 channels.
conv3_block12_concat.analogy: Like building a sandwich where each layer adds a new slice, keeping all previous slices intact.
conv3_block12_concat.whyItMatters: Preserves features directly across all subsequent block stages.
conv3_block12_concat.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.

pool3_conv.detailed: Compresses channel volume by 50% to prevent channel build-up across dense stages, managing memory constraints.
pool3_conv.keyTakeaway: Compression prevents the channel count from blowing up.

pool3_pool.summary: Reduces spatial size using average pooling.
pool3_pool.detailed: Average pooling downsamples spatial grids by 50% to reduce resolution.
pool3_pool.whyItMatters: Saves compute sizes for the next block.
pool3_pool.keyTakeaway: Average pooling maps features to lower grid coordinates.

conv5_block32_2_conv.detailed: The growth rate (k) is 32. This layer only needs to output 32 channels, representing the new features discovered.
conv5_block32_2_conv.whyItMatters: Controls model size while adding new visual representations.
conv5_block32_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Growth rate (k) defines how many channels each layer contributes.

conv5_block32_concat.detailed: Concatenates the block's input (1888 channels) with the new 32 channels produced by the 3x3 conv, outputting 1920 channels.
conv5_block32_concat.analogy: Like building a sandwich where each layer adds a new slice, keeping all previous slices intact.
conv5_block32_concat.whyItMatters: Preserves features directly across all subsequent block stages.
conv5_block32_concat.keyTakeaway: Concatenation preserves all visual features for subsequent reuse.

bn.detailed: Pre-activation setup requires a final BN layer before pooling.
bn.whyItMatters: Ensures balanced scaling of final outputs.

relu.whyItMatters: Prepares activated representation for global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling averaging spatial channels.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 grid into a single feature vector of 1920 elements.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

block1a_dwconv.detailed: Applies independent kernels over each channel to model spatial details.

block1a_se_squeeze.detailed: Collapses spatial dimensions to form channel global context vectors.
block1a_se_squeeze.whyItMatters: Used to model interdependencies between channels.
block1a_se_squeeze.keyTakeaway: Collapses spatial details to channel context.

block1a_se_reshape.detailed: Compresses channel features to 8 with bias.

block1a_se_expand.summary: SE expand layer with sigmoid activation.
block1a_se_expand.detailed: Expands bottleneck back to output scales.
block1a_se_expand.whyItMatters: Gives weights representing importance of channels.

block1a_se_excite.detailed: Multiplies feature maps with SE weights.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

block1a_dwconv.detailed: Applies independent kernels over each channel to model spatial details.

block1a_se_squeeze.detailed: Collapses spatial dimensions to form channel global context vectors.
block1a_se_squeeze.whyItMatters: Used to model interdependencies between channels.
block1a_se_squeeze.keyTakeaway: Collapses spatial details to channel context.

block1a_se_reshape.detailed: Compresses channel features to 12 with bias.

block1a_se_expand.summary: SE expand layer with sigmoid activation.
block1a_se_expand.detailed: Expands bottleneck back to output scales.
block1a_se_expand.whyItMatters: Gives weights representing importance of channels.

block1a_se_excite.detailed: Multiplies feature maps with SE weights.

block1a_project_conv.detailed: Compresses channel features back to 24 dimensions.
block1a_project_conv.whyItMatters: Linear bottlenecks prevent activation information losses.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

top_conv.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Global average pooling.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

normalization.summary: Performs dynamic rescaling and normalization of input pixels.
normalization.detailed: Scales and normalizes input image pixel ranges to match train bounds.
normalization.whyItMatters: Prevents gradient saturation on early convolutions.

stem_padding.summary: Zero padding for the stem conv.

stem_conv.detailed: Captures baseline visual primitives and shrinks resolution by 50%.
stem_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks image bounds early to reduce compute scales.

stem_activation.detailed: Swish: x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic curve that outperforms ReLU on deep models.

global_average_pooling2d.detailed: Averages visual representations to a single vector.
global_average_pooling2d.whyItMatters: Reduces parameter scale before dense projection.

predictions.summary: Softmax predictions.
predictions.detailed: Maps embeddings to class logits.
predictions.whyItMatters: Decision classifier head.

parameter_alignment_adjustment.detailed: Represents the parameter difference (20,807,872) between the visualizer's simplified layer routing graph and the official Keras Applications parameter count.
parameter_alignment_adjustment.whyItMatters: Ensures model-level parameter math matches the sum of the individual layers exactly.
parameter_alignment_adjustment.keyTakeaway: Aligns the visual topology weights with Keras Applications measurements.

input_1.summary: Input layer for grayscale digits.
input_1.detailed: Accepts 32x32 pixel grayscale images. Yann LeCun padded the original 28x28 MNIST digits to 32x32 so that potential features could be centered.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Padding inputs allows early convolutions to extract boundary information cleanly.

conv2d_1.detailed: Uses 6 distinct 5x5 convolutional filters. Since padding is 'valid', the spatial height/width shrinks from 32 to 28.
conv2d_1.keyTakeaway: Valid padding naturally shrinks spatial dimensions.

avg_pool_1.detailed: Average pooling calculates the mean values over 2x2 grids, reducing spatial dimensions to 14x14 while keeping the 6 channels constant.

conv2d_2.detailed: Computes 16 filters of size 5x5 over the 14x14 features. Pushes the network to learn combinations of low-level features.
conv2d_2.keyTakeaway: Deeper convolutional layers combine simple features into complex representations.

avg_pool_2.detailed: Halves the height and width from 10 to 5. Keeps parameter count low before entering fully connected segments.
avg_pool_2.keyTakeaway: Downsampling prevents fully connected layers from overfitting.

flatten_1.detailed: Rearranges the 5x5x16 feature maps into a single flat vector of length 400 to serve as input for classification layers.
flatten_1.keyTakeaway: Flattening bridges spatial representations and categorical classifiers.

dense_1.detailed: Applies a full dot product calculation. Tanh activation maps outputs between -1 and 1.
dense_1.keyTakeaway: Fully connected layers map learned features to global classifications.

dense_2.summary: Second dense layer mapping to digit features.
dense_2.detailed: Reduces dimensionality from 120 to 84, preparing for the final digit classification mapping.
dense_2.keyTakeaway: Intermediate dense layers squeeze classifications into core classes.

output.detailed: Converts final raw scores into a probability distribution summing to 1.0 across the 10 numeric digits.
output.keyTakeaway: Softmax enables model outputs to be interpreted as probabilities.

input_1.summary: RGB image inputs for the MaxViT network.
input_1.detailed: Accepts standard 224x224 pixels RGB color images. Sourced from the tf.keras.applications.MaxViT API.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Processes standard color images.

stem_conv1.detailed: Uses 3x3 conv with stride 2 to quickly reduce spatial dimensions, lowering overall compute complexity.
stem_conv1.keyTakeaway: Quick spatial reduction keeps downstream compute loads small.

stem_conv2.detailed: Captures fine local edge and texture groupings before downsampling to Stage 1.

stage1_blocks.summary: First hybrid stage combining conv and dual attention.
stage1_blocks.detailed: Downsamples maps to 56x56. Applies Block Attention (W-MSA inside 7x7 windows) followed by Grid Attention (G-MSA across sparse 7x7 grids). Together, they capture both local details and global relationships.
stage1_blocks.keyTakeaway: Multi-axis attention (Block + Grid) provides global attention coverage in linear compute time.

stage2_blocks.detailed: Constructs multi-scale representations. The MBConv downsamples inputs, while the dual attention blocks process channel relationships at 28x28 scale.

stage3_blocks.detailed: The core representative bottleneck stage. Combines convolution and attention, processing semantic structures at 14x14 resolution.
stage3_blocks.keyTakeaway: Extracts complex object parts and textures, matching standard deep block structures.

stage4_blocks.detailed: Processes high-level semantic representation at final 7x7 grid size before classification.

norm_out.detailed: Stabilizes embeddings across the 512 channel dimensions.

avg_pool.summary: Collapses spatial dimensions to 1D vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages final activations across the 7x7 grid to compile a 512-dimensional vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Aggregates final visual features globally.

output.summary: Softmax conversion mapping logits to probabilities.
output.detailed: Converts visual category logits into probability distributions.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

conv1.summary: Standard early 3x3 convolution to downsample inputs.
conv1.detailed: Captures initial visual patterns like lines and edges. Stride of 2 halves resolution to reduce downstream processing.
conv1.whyItMatters: Early spatial reduction saves computation in deeper stages.
conv1.keyTakeaway: Early layers capture general visual contours.

conv1_relu.detailed: Capping values to 6 prevents numerical overflow and is optimal for fixed-point integer quantization on edge hardware.
conv1_relu.whyItMatters: Essential for deployment on low-precision microcontrollers and mobile processors.
conv1_relu.keyTakeaway: Capped activations prevent precision loss during quantization.

conv_dw_1.detailed: Operates spatial convolving over each of the 32 channels separately. Cuts down computing weights by 90% compared to standard convolutions.
conv_dw_1.whyItMatters: The core mechanism behind MobileNet's fast runtime and low parameter size.
conv_dw_1.keyTakeaway: Depthwise convolutions perform spatial filtering without mixing channels.

conv_dw_1_bn.detailed: Stabilizes the activation scale before non-linear gating.
conv_dw_1_bn.whyItMatters: Enables faster and more robust gradient updates.

conv_dw_1_relu.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to depthwise outputs.
conv_dw_1_relu.whyItMatters: Controls numerical ranges for embedded hardware.

conv_pw_1.detailed: Performs linear combinations over the spatial outputs to project channels from 32 to 64.
conv_pw_1.whyItMatters: Handles the cross-channel feature integration step in depthwise-separable conv blocks.

conv_pw_1_bn.detailed: Normalizes the mixed representation before activation.

conv_pw_1_relu.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to pointwise outputs.
conv_pw_1_relu.whyItMatters: Final non-linear gating of block output.

conv_pw_10.detailed: Performs linear combinations over the spatial outputs to project channels from 512 to 512.
conv_pw_10.whyItMatters: Handles the cross-channel feature integration step in depthwise-separable conv blocks.

conv_pw_10_bn.detailed: Normalizes the mixed representation before activation.

conv_pw_10_relu.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to pointwise outputs.
conv_pw_10_relu.whyItMatters: Final non-linear gating of block output.

conv_dw_11.detailed: Operates spatial convolving over each of the 512 channels separately. Cuts down computing weights by 90% compared to standard convolutions.
conv_dw_11.whyItMatters: The core mechanism behind MobileNet's fast runtime and low parameter size.
conv_dw_11.keyTakeaway: Depthwise convolutions perform spatial filtering without mixing channels.

conv_dw_11_bn.detailed: Stabilizes the activation scale before non-linear gating.
conv_dw_11_bn.whyItMatters: Enables faster and more robust gradient updates.

global_average_pooling2d.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions (7x7) to form a global feature vector.
global_average_pooling2d.whyItMatters: Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
global_average_pooling2d.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

Conv1.summary: Standard early 3x3 convolution to downsample inputs.
Conv1.detailed: Computes 32 feature maps by sliding 3x3 filters with a stride of 2 across the raw RGB channels to capture early colors and edges.
Conv1.whyItMatters: Shrinks inputs early to decrease computational costs downstream.
Conv1.keyTakeaway: Early layers extract basic visual primitives (edges, shapes).

Conv1_relu.detailed: Calculates f(x) = min(max(0, x), 6). Capping the activation at 6 prevents values from expanding infinitely, which preserves precision during fixed-point quantization on edge devices.
Conv1_relu.whyItMatters: Critical for low-power mobile CPU and DSP quantization compatibility.
Conv1_relu.keyTakeaway: ReLU6 restricts activation range to support lightweight integer quantization.

block_1_depthwise.detailed: Applies a single 3x3 kernel per input channel. Standard convolutions mix spatial and channel details at the same time; depthwise convs only slide spatially. Saves over 90% parameter overhead compared to standard convs.
block_1_depthwise.whyItMatters: The core mechanism behind MobileNet's fast runtime and low parameter size.
block_1_depthwise.keyTakeaway: Depthwise convolutions perform spatial filtering without mixing channels.

block_1_depthwise_relu.summary: Applies ReLU6 activation to depthwise outputs.

block_1_project.detailed: Compresses 32 channels back to 16. Crucially, this layer does NOT apply a non-linear activation (it is linear). Non-linear activations on narrow channels throw away valuable visual information.
block_1_project.whyItMatters: Preserves information capacity in low-dimensional spaces.
block_1_project.keyTakeaway: Linear bottlenecks prevent activation loss in compact channels.

block_2_expand.summary: 1x1 Expansion convolution projecting to higher dims.
block_2_expand.detailed: Projects input from 16 to a higher-dimensional space of 96 channels. Expands capacity so the subsequent depthwise conv can process complex filters.
block_2_expand.whyItMatters: MobileNetV2 uses an 'Inverted Bottleneck' structure where filters are expanded first, then compressed.
block_2_expand.keyTakeaway: Expansion makes learning high-dimensional manifolds easier.

block_2_depthwise.detailed: Applies a single 3x3 kernel per input channel. Standard convolutions mix spatial and channel details at the same time; depthwise convs only slide spatially. Saves over 90% parameter overhead compared to standard convs.

Conv_1.summary: 1x1 projection expanding visual features before pooling.
Conv_1.detailed: Aggregates features to 1,280 channels before pooling to enrich the global visual classifier descriptor.

global_average_pooling2d.summary: Collapses 7x7 spatial layout into a global channel average vector.
global_average_pooling2d.detailed: Averages pixel coordinates across each channel to form a final dense vector, reducing overfitting constraints.
global_average_pooling2d.keyTakeaway: GAP drastically restricts parameter weights size.

predictions.summary: Dense predictions softmax layer resolving ImageNet classes.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

conv1.detailed: Extracts initial features while halving resolution to 112x112.
conv1.whyItMatters: Reduces computational demands in initial stages.

conv1_bn.whyItMatters: Enables faster and more robust gradient updates.

conv1_act.detailed: Hard-swish approximates swish activation: x * relu6(x+3)/6, which is faster on mobile platforms.
conv1_act.whyItMatters: Combines non-linearity of ReLU with smooth curves of Swish to enhance gradient flows.
conv1_act.keyTakeaway: Approximates swish activation for fast mobile processing.

input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: Slides 64 different 7x7 kernels with a stride of 2 to extract general contours while shrinking spatial grid sizes.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks resolution from 224x224 to 112x112, reducing downstream computation.
conv1_conv.keyTakeaway: Early pooling and large stride convolutions preserve core shapes while minimizing compute requirements.

conv1_bn.detailed: Batch Normalization shifts activations to mean 0 and variance 1 across batch dimensions to avoid internal covariate shifts.
conv1_bn.whyItMatters: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.
conv1_bn.keyTakeaway: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.

conv1_relu.detailed: Calculates f(x) = max(0, x) element-wise. Keeps active regions positive and sets negative features to zero.
conv1_relu.whyItMatters: ReLU is computationally simple and prevents gradient saturation for positive inputs.
conv1_relu.keyTakeaway: ReLU allows learning complex non-linear combinations of edges.

pool1_pad.summary: Pads borders to control MaxPooling dimensions.
pool1_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around the 112x112 feature map to expand it to 114x114, ensuring MaxPooling dimensions fit correctly.
pool1_pad.keyTakeaway: Zero padding avoids border information loss.

pool1_pool.detailed: Extracts the maximum active pixel in overlapping 3x3 cells with stride 2.
pool1_pool.whyItMatters: Reduces downstream spatial dims and introduces local translation invariance.
pool1_pool.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling reduces resolution while filtering peak activations.

conv2_block1_1_conv.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
conv2_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Controls computation scale in deep bottleneck blocks.
conv2_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

conv2_block1_1_bn.detailed: Normalizes the compressed activation space before applying spatial convolutions.
conv2_block1_1_bn.whyItMatters: Ensures inputs to downstream spatial convolutions are scaled identically.
conv2_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.

conv2_block1_2_conv.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. Stride of 1 handles spatial downsampling.
conv2_block1_2_conv.whyItMatters: Combines spatial and channel features on the compressed bottleneck representation.
conv2_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

conv2_block1_2_bn.detailed: Scales features before the final dimension projection.

conv2_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
conv2_block1_3_conv.whyItMatters: Prepares the tensor size to match the original skip connection shape.
conv2_block1_3_conv.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

conv2_block1_0_conv.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 256 for element-wise addition.
conv2_block1_0_conv.whyItMatters: Required to project spatial and channel dimensions.
conv2_block1_0_conv.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

conv2_block1_0_bn.detailed: Applies Batch Normalization to the shortcut projection branch.
conv2_block1_0_bn.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across shortcut outputs.

conv2_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv2_block1_add.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
conv2_block1_add.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
conv2_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

conv2_block1_out.detailed: Rectifies elements to introduce non-linearity at the block output.
conv2_block1_out.whyItMatters: Prepares activated features for the next block.
conv2_block1_out.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Computes the spatial average of each of the 2,048 feature channels. Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

conv1_pad.summary: Zero padding for the large conv1 kernel.
conv1_pad.detailed: Pads borders with zeros to keep convolution dimensions matching standard Keras shapes.
conv1_pad.whyItMatters: Required to properly shape spatial grids in stem layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: First spatial feature extractor, shrinking inputs to 112x112.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Captures general contours while saving computing costs.

pool1_pad.detailed: Pads input features before max pooling to fit spatial shapes.

pool1_pool.detailed: Filters feature map to reduce resolution to 56x56.
pool1_pool.whyItMatters: Extracts translation invariant features and drops compute size.

post_bn.detailed: Pre-activation architectures add a final BN to normalize feature maps before pooling.

post_relu.detailed: Locks final features as active positive values before average pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 channel representation to a single vector, regularizing representations.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: Slides 64 different 7x7 kernels with a stride of 2 to extract general contours while shrinking spatial grid sizes.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks resolution from 224x224 to 112x112, reducing downstream computation.
conv1_conv.keyTakeaway: Early pooling and large stride convolutions preserve core shapes while minimizing compute requirements.

conv1_bn.detailed: Batch Normalization shifts activations to mean 0 and variance 1 across batch dimensions to avoid internal covariate shifts.
conv1_bn.whyItMatters: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.
conv1_bn.keyTakeaway: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.

conv1_relu.detailed: Calculates f(x) = max(0, x) element-wise. Keeps active regions positive and sets negative features to zero.
conv1_relu.whyItMatters: ReLU is computationally simple and prevents gradient saturation for positive inputs.
conv1_relu.keyTakeaway: ReLU allows learning complex non-linear combinations of edges.

pool1_pad.summary: Pads borders to control MaxPooling dimensions.
pool1_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around the 112x112 feature map to expand it to 114x114, ensuring MaxPooling dimensions fit correctly.
pool1_pad.keyTakeaway: Zero padding avoids border information loss.

pool1_pool.detailed: Extracts the maximum active pixel in overlapping 3x3 cells with stride 2.
pool1_pool.whyItMatters: Reduces downstream spatial dims and introduces local translation invariance.
pool1_pool.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling reduces resolution while filtering peak activations.

conv2_block1_1_conv.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
conv2_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Controls computation scale in deep bottleneck blocks.
conv2_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

conv2_block1_1_bn.detailed: Normalizes the compressed activation space before applying spatial convolutions.
conv2_block1_1_bn.whyItMatters: Ensures inputs to downstream spatial convolutions are scaled identically.
conv2_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.

conv2_block1_2_conv.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. Stride of 1 handles spatial downsampling.
conv2_block1_2_conv.whyItMatters: Combines spatial and channel features on the compressed bottleneck representation.
conv2_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

conv2_block1_2_bn.detailed: Scales features before the final dimension projection.

conv2_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
conv2_block1_3_conv.whyItMatters: Prepares the tensor size to match the original skip connection shape.
conv2_block1_3_conv.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

conv2_block1_0_conv.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 256 for element-wise addition.
conv2_block1_0_conv.whyItMatters: Required to project spatial and channel dimensions.
conv2_block1_0_conv.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

conv2_block1_0_bn.detailed: Applies Batch Normalization to the shortcut projection branch.
conv2_block1_0_bn.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across shortcut outputs.

conv2_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv2_block1_add.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
conv2_block1_add.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
conv2_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

conv2_block1_out.detailed: Rectifies elements to introduce non-linearity at the block output.
conv2_block1_out.whyItMatters: Prepares activated features for the next block.
conv2_block1_out.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

conv5_block1_2_conv.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. Stride of 2 handles spatial downsampling.
conv5_block1_2_conv.whyItMatters: Combines spatial and channel features on the compressed bottleneck representation.
conv5_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

conv5_block1_2_bn.detailed: Scales features before the final dimension projection.

conv5_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 512 channels back to 2048 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
conv5_block1_3_conv.whyItMatters: Prepares the tensor size to match the original skip connection shape.
conv5_block1_3_conv.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

conv5_block1_0_conv.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 2048 for element-wise addition.
conv5_block1_0_conv.whyItMatters: Required to project spatial and channel dimensions.
conv5_block1_0_conv.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

conv5_block1_0_bn.detailed: Applies Batch Normalization to the shortcut projection branch.
conv5_block1_0_bn.whyItMatters: Maintains normal variance across shortcut outputs.

conv5_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv5_block1_add.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
conv5_block1_add.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
conv5_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

conv5_block1_out.detailed: Rectifies elements to introduce non-linearity at the block output.
conv5_block1_out.whyItMatters: Prepares activated features for the next block.
conv5_block1_out.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

conv5_block2_1_conv.whyItMatters: Controls computation scale in deep bottleneck blocks.
conv5_block2_1_conv.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Computes the spatial average of each of the 2,048 feature channels. Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

conv1_pad.summary: Zero padding for the large conv1 kernel.
conv1_pad.detailed: Pads borders with zeros to keep convolution dimensions matching standard Keras shapes.
conv1_pad.whyItMatters: Required to properly shape spatial grids in stem layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: First spatial feature extractor, shrinking inputs to 112x112.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Captures general contours while saving computing costs.

pool1_pad.detailed: Pads input features before max pooling to fit spatial shapes.

pool1_pool.detailed: Filters feature map to reduce resolution to 56x56.
pool1_pool.whyItMatters: Extracts translation invariant features and drops compute size.

conv2_block1_preact_bn.whyItMatters: The core V2 enhancement that guarantees identity skip paths.
conv2_block1_preact_bn.keyTakeaway: Normalizing inputs before convolution yields smoother updates.

conv2_block1_preact_relu.detailed: Activates the inputs before projection and convolution branches.
conv2_block1_preact_relu.whyItMatters: Permits full non-linear learning representation prior to convolutional mapping.

conv2_block1_2_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around features before spatial filtering.

conv2_block1_2_relu.detailed: Non-linear mapping prior to channel expansion.

post_bn.detailed: Pre-activation architectures add a final BN to normalize feature maps before pooling.

post_relu.detailed: Locks final features as active positive values before average pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 channel representation to a single vector, regularizing representations.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: Slides 64 different 7x7 kernels with a stride of 2 to extract general contours while shrinking spatial grid sizes.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Shrinks resolution from 224x224 to 112x112, reducing downstream computation.
conv1_conv.keyTakeaway: Early pooling and large stride convolutions preserve core shapes while minimizing compute requirements.

conv1_bn.detailed: Batch Normalization shifts activations to mean 0 and variance 1 across batch dimensions to avoid internal covariate shifts.
conv1_bn.keyTakeaway: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.

conv1_relu.detailed: Calculates f(x) = max(0, x) element-wise. Keeps active regions positive and sets negative features to zero.
conv1_relu.keyTakeaway: ReLU allows learning complex non-linear combinations of edges.

pool1_pad.summary: Pads borders to control MaxPooling dimensions.
pool1_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around the 112x112 feature map to expand it to 114x114, ensuring MaxPooling dimensions fit correctly.
pool1_pad.keyTakeaway: Zero padding avoids border information loss.

pool1_pool.detailed: Extracts the maximum active pixel in overlapping 3x3 cells with stride 2.
pool1_pool.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling reduces resolution while filtering peak activations.

conv2_block1_1_conv.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
conv2_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Controls computation scales in deep bottleneck sandwiches.
conv2_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

conv2_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.

conv2_block1_2_conv.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. If stride is 2, it reduces spatial height and width by half.
conv2_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

conv2_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
conv2_block1_3_conv.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

conv2_block1_0_conv.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 256 for element-wise addition.
conv2_block1_0_conv.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

conv2_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv2_block1_add.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
conv2_block1_add.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
conv2_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

conv2_block1_out.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

conv5_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Controls computation scales in deep bottleneck sandwiches.
conv5_block1_1_conv.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

conv5_block1_1_bn.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.

conv5_block1_2_conv.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. If stride is 2, it reduces spatial height and width by half.
conv5_block1_2_conv.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

conv5_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 512 channels back to 2048 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
conv5_block1_3_conv.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

conv5_block1_0_conv.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 2048 for element-wise addition.
conv5_block1_0_conv.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

conv5_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv5_block1_add.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
conv5_block1_add.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
conv5_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.

conv5_block1_out.keyTakeaway: Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Computes the spatial average of each of the 2,048 feature channels. Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

conv1_pad.summary: Zero padding for the large conv1 kernel.
conv1_pad.detailed: Pads borders with zeros to keep convolution dimensions matching standard Keras shapes.
conv1_pad.whyItMatters: Required to properly shape spatial grids in stem layers.

conv1_conv.detailed: First spatial feature extractor, shrinking inputs to 112x112.
conv1_conv.whyItMatters: Captures general contours while saving computing costs.

pool1_pad.detailed: Pads input features before max pooling to fit spatial shapes.

pool1_pool.detailed: Filters feature map to reduce resolution to 56x56.
pool1_pool.whyItMatters: Extracts translation invariant features and drops compute size.

conv2_block1_preact_bn.detailed: Normalizes block inputs before applying convolutions, stabilizing gradient updates.
conv2_block1_preact_bn.whyItMatters: The core V2 enhancement that guarantees identity skip paths.
conv2_block1_preact_bn.keyTakeaway: Normalizing inputs before convolution yields smoother updates.

conv2_block1_preact_relu.detailed: Activates the inputs before projection and convolution branches.
conv2_block1_preact_relu.whyItMatters: Permits full non-linear learning representation prior to convolutional mapping.

conv2_block1_0_conv.summary: Shortcut projection mapping channels and grids.

conv2_block1_0_conv.whyItMatters: Synchronizes feature shapes for residual add.

conv2_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Saves computations prior to the spatial convolution.

conv2_block1_1_relu.detailed: Rectifies features to prepare for spatial convolutions.

conv2_block1_2_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around features before spatial filtering.

conv2_block1_2_conv.detailed: Convolves 3x3 kernel over spatial grids to extract patterns. Performs downsampling if stride=2.
conv2_block1_2_conv.whyItMatters: Core feature extractor layer in ResNet block.

conv2_block1_2_relu.detailed: Non-linear mapping prior to channel expansion.

conv2_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels.

conv2_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv2_block1_add.detailed: Combines the block input directly with the main residual path. The output is unactivated, preserving clean gradient paths.
conv2_block1_add.whyItMatters: V2 identity connections propagate gradients directly across blocks without non-linear gating.
conv2_block1_add.keyTakeaway: Direct identity summation improves backpropagation flow.

conv5_block1_preact_bn.whyItMatters: The core V2 enhancement that guarantees identity skip paths.
conv5_block1_preact_bn.keyTakeaway: Normalizing inputs before convolution yields smoother updates.

conv5_block1_preact_relu.detailed: Activates the inputs before projection and convolution branches.
conv5_block1_preact_relu.whyItMatters: Permits full non-linear learning representation prior to convolutional mapping.

conv5_block1_0_conv.summary: Shortcut projection mapping channels and grids.
conv5_block1_0_conv.detailed: Outputs matched channel dimensions using 1x1 conv over pre-activated inputs.
conv5_block1_0_conv.whyItMatters: Synchronizes feature shapes for residual add.

conv5_block1_1_conv.detailed: Compresses channels to bottleneck dimensions, preserving spatial features.
conv5_block1_1_conv.whyItMatters: Saves computations prior to the spatial convolution.

conv5_block1_1_relu.detailed: Rectifies features to prepare for spatial convolutions.

conv5_block1_2_pad.detailed: Adds zero borders around features before spatial filtering.

conv5_block1_2_conv.detailed: Convolves 3x3 kernel over spatial grids to extract patterns. Performs downsampling if stride=2.

conv5_block1_2_relu.detailed: Non-linear mapping prior to channel expansion.

conv5_block1_3_conv.detailed: Expands output from 512 channels back to 2048 channels.

conv5_block1_add.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
conv5_block1_add.detailed: Combines the block input directly with the main residual path. The output is unactivated, preserving clean gradient paths.
conv5_block1_add.whyItMatters: V2 identity connections propagate gradients directly across blocks without non-linear gating.

post_bn.detailed: Pre-activation architectures add a final BN to normalize feature maps before pooling.

post_relu.detailed: Locks final features as active positive values before average pooling.

avg_pool.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages each 7x7 channel representation to a single vector, regularizing representations.
avg_pool.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

predictions.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
predictions.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
predictions.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

input_1.summary: RGB image inputs for the Swin Network.
input_1.detailed: Accepts 224x224 pixel color images. The Swin architecture partitions this image into a hierarchical series of patches to construct multi-scale representations.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Swin uses standard RGB images, processing local features first before aggregating.

patch_partition.detailed: Processes the image using a 4x4 convolution with stride 4. This reduces the spatial dimensions to 56x56 while establishing a starting embedding size of 96.
patch_partition.keyTakeaway: Stem acts as a patch partition, producing the finest scale token map (56x56).

stage1_blocks.summary: Local & Shifted window attention at 56x56 resolution.
stage1_blocks.detailed: Applies Local Window Attention (W-MSA) in the first block, dividing the 56x56 grid into 8x8 local windows. The second block applies Shifted Window Attention (SW-MSA), moving the windows by 4 pixels to enable cross-window information flow.
stage1_blocks.keyTakeaway: Shifted windows allow local self-attention to share info globally over successive blocks.

patch_merging1.summary: Halves spatial resolution while doubling channels.
patch_merging1.detailed: Groups 2x2 neighboring patches together to downsample resolution by 2x. A linear projection then reduces the concatenated channels (384) to 192, mimicking a CNN stride downsample.
patch_merging1.keyTakeaway: Patch merging constructs the hierarchical representation, decreasing size from 56x56 to 28x28.

stage2_blocks.summary: Applies W-MSA and SW-MSA at 28x28 scale.
stage2_blocks.detailed: Processes the 28x28 token map. Self-attention is still computed within local 8x8 windows, ensuring compute cost scales linearly, not quadratically, with spatial size.
stage2_blocks.keyTakeaway: Local attention windows remain constant (8x8) as the resolution shrinks, making computations efficient.

patch_merging2.summary: Reduces map size to 14x14 while doubling features.
patch_merging2.detailed: Downsamples the token map to 14x14. Prepares Swin representation for the deep feature-extraction blocks of Stage 3.
patch_merging2.keyTakeaway: Acts as the hierarchical bottleneck step before the deep Stage 3.

stage3_blocks.detailed: Swin's heavy extraction bottleneck containing 6 transformer blocks. Alternate blocks apply W-MSA and SW-MSA attention to refine deep global-local relationships.
stage3_blocks.keyTakeaway: Swin's Stage 3 contains the majority of the network layers and parameters, similar to ResNet's Conv4 block.

patch_merging3.summary: Reduces map size to final 7x7 resolution.
patch_merging3.detailed: Final spatial downsampling step. Combines tokens into a final coarse 7x7 grid representing high-level spatial blocks.
patch_merging3.keyTakeaway: Creates final token resolution of 7x7, matching typical CNN classifier input dimensions.

stage4_blocks.detailed: Final two transformer blocks operating at 7x7 token resolution. Since spatial size is small (49 tokens), global attention is naturally simulated here.
stage4_blocks.keyTakeaway: Extracts semantic attributes from high-dimensional token interactions.

norm_out.detailed: Ensures all final token representations are normalized along the embedding dimension before pooling is applied.
norm_out.keyTakeaway: Final LayerNorm ensures features are clean for linear classification.

avg_pool.summary: Averages the 7x7 spatial tokens into a single vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages token vectors across the spatial grid to construct a single 768-dimensional image feature vector.
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Reduces 3D tensor coordinates into a 1D classification vector.

head.summary: Dense mapping to class output logits.

output.detailed: Converts final predictions to class probabilities summing to 1.0.

input_1.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
input_1.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
input_1.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the model.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

block1_conv1.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block1_conv2.detailed: Slides 64 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block1_pool.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
block1_pool.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
block1_pool.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

block2_conv1.detailed: Slides 128 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block2_conv2.detailed: Slides 128 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block2_pool.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

block4_conv1.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block4_conv2.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block4_conv3.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block4_pool.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.

block4_pool.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

block5_conv1.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block5_conv2.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block5_conv3.detailed: Slides 512 filters across input features to extract local patterns (like curves and edges).

block5_pool.summary: Halves spatial resolution while retaining peak features.
block5_pool.detailed: Slides a 2x2 window with stride 2 over each feature map, outputting the maximum value in that cell. Downsamples resolution, decreasing compute while preserving dominant signals.
block5_pool.keyTakeaway: Pooling achieves downsampling and feature invariance with zero learnable parameters.

flatten.summary: Unrolls spatial features into a 1D vector.
flatten.detailed: Converts the 3D tensor of shape 7x7x512 into a single vector of length 25088. This step transitions the model from spatial feature mapping to multi-class classification reasoning.
flatten.whyItMatters: Bridges local convolutions and dense classifiers.
flatten.keyTakeaway: Flattening discards spatial dimensions while preserving the raw activation vector.

fc1.detailed: This single layer accounts for over 70% of VGG's total parameter count. It performs global visual reasoning across the entire set of flattened features, determining how parts relate to classes.
fc1.whyItMatters: The massive parameter footprint of this transition is why modern models prefer Global Average Pooling over Flattening.
fc1.keyTakeaway: Fully connected layers contain the majority of standard CNN parameters.

fc2.summary: Second dense layer representing high-level semantic abstractions.
fc2.detailed: Performs further non-linear mapping on the 4096-dimensional global visual representations, building high-level classification templates.
fc2.whyItMatters: Deep fully connected layers learn abstract visual classification features.

predictions.summary: Softmax classification output layer.
predictions.detailed: The final network stage. Converts learned features into a probability distribution over 1000 ImageNet categories.
predictions.whyItMatters: Provides interpretable confidence scores representing the model's decisions.
predictions.keyTakeaway: Softmax maps outputs to a valid probability distribution that sums to 1.
