input_1.summary: Input layer for color images.
input_1.detailed: Accepts 224x224 pixels RGB images. Unlike CNNs, which process pixels directly, ViT will divide this image into 16x16 non-overlapping patches.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Standard input size for ViT-Base is 224x224, matching standard CNN benchmarks.

patch_proj.summary: Transforms image patches into token vectors.
patch_proj.detailed: Uses a 16x16 2D convolution with stride 16. This extracts 14x14 = 196 patches and projects each patch into a 768-dimensional embedding, acting as 'visual tokens'.
patch_proj.keyTakeaway: Patch projection translates spatial pixels into sequence tokens for the Transformer.

flatten_patches.detailed: Flattens the 14x14 spatial grid of patches into a sequence of 196 patch tokens, making it compatible with standard sequence encoders.
flatten_patches.keyTakeaway: Sequence length is fixed at 196 (plus an optional CLS class token).

pos_embed.summary: Adds spatial coordinates to sequence tokens.
pos_embed.detailed: Since Self-Attention is permutation invariant, it ignores sequence order. Learnable position embeddings are added to restore spatial layout awareness.
pos_embed.keyTakeaway: Position embeddings are critical for retaining the spatial topology of the image.

block0_ln1.detailed: Layer Normalization stabilizes the hidden states by computing the mean and variance across the feature dimension (768) independently for each token.
block0_ln1.keyTakeaway: LayerNorm is applied before self-attention (pre-LN style) in modern ViTs to ensure training stability.

block0_msa.summary: Enables global context sharing between all tokens.
block0_msa.detailed: Uses 12 attention heads. Computes Query-Key similarity dot-products, creating an attention matrix. Tokens weight and retrieve information from all other tokens globally, ignoring spatial distances.
block0_msa.keyTakeaway: Self-attention replaces convolution's local receptive field with a global receptivity in every layer.

block0_add1.detailed: Adds the input of the LN+Attention block to its output: x = x + Attention(LN(x)). Ensures gradients flow directly backward through the layers.
block0_add1.keyTakeaway: Prevents vanishing gradients across deep stacks of transformer encoder layers.

block0_ln2.summary: Normalizes states before the MLP block.
block0_ln2.detailed: Normalizes states across the embedding dimension to prepare inputs for the high-dimensional non-linear projections of the MLP layer.
block0_ln2.keyTakeaway: LayerNorm ensures stable training dynamics before MLP expansion.

block0_mlp_dense1.detailed: Projects embeddings to a 4x higher dimensional space (3072). This allows features to be linearly separated and combined through non-linear activations.
block0_mlp_dense1.keyTakeaway: MLP blocks operate on each token embedding independently (channel-wise mixing).

block0_mlp_act.detailed: GELU (Gaussian Error Linear Unit) scales inputs by their cumulative probability distribution, providing a smoother gradient flow than ReLU.
block0_mlp_act.keyTakeaway: GELU is the standard activation function in modern vision and text transformer blocks.

block0_mlp_dense2.summary: Compresses MLP representations back to core embedding size.
block0_mlp_dense2.detailed: Maps the expanded features back to the main sequence dimension (768), ensuring output token shapes match skip shortcut dimensions.
block0_mlp_dense2.keyTakeaway: Prepares MLP representations for residual summation.

block0_add2.summary: Second residual skip connection around MLP block.
block0_add2.detailed: Summates the attention representation (from skip 1) with the compressed MLP output: x = x + MLP(LN(x)).
block0_add2.keyTakeaway: Bypasses MLP layers to maintain stable backprop flow.

blocks_stack.summary: A stack of 11 identical encoder blocks.
blocks_stack.detailed: Repeatedly processes sequence relationships. Each block contains self-attention and MLP sub-layers. Deep layers refine semantic features of token interactions.
blocks_stack.keyTakeaway: ViT-Base stacks a total of 12 transformer encoder blocks for deep representation learning.

norm_out.detailed: Stabilizes the final output states of the Transformer encoder before mapping to categorical logits.
norm_out.keyTakeaway: Final LayerNorm ensures scale consistency before the classifier.

avg_pool.summary: Collapses sequence dimensions to a single vector.
avg_pool.detailed: Averages representations across all 196 sequence tokens to construct a unified image representation vector. (Note: Sourced from Keras ViT, which uses average pooling over sequence instead of the standard CLS token).
avg_pool.keyTakeaway: Collapses spatial/sequence dimension to create a global class embedding.

head.summary: Dense mapping to class index logits.
head.detailed: Final fully connected classification layer predicting categorical scores.
head.keyTakeaway: Predicts raw log-odds (logits) for the classifier.

output.detailed: Softmax maps logits to positive values summing to 1.0, representing the model's prediction confidence score for each class.
output.keyTakeaway: Yields output confidence distribution over ImageNet classes.

input_1.summary: RGB input image.
input_1.detailed: Entry input resolution.
input_1.whyItMatters: Input stage.
input_1.keyTakeaway: Image entrance.

block1_conv1_conv.detailed: Slides 32 filters of size 3x3 across the input.

block1_conv1_bn.detailed: Normalizes channel activations to stabilize learning.
block1_conv1_bn.whyItMatters: Prevents vanishing gradients in deep networks.

block1_conv1_act.detailed: Adds non-linearity to the network representation.

block1_conv2_conv.detailed: Slides 64 filters of size 3x3 across the input.

block1_conv2_bn.detailed: Normalizes channel activations to stabilize learning.
block1_conv2_bn.whyItMatters: Prevents vanishing gradients in deep networks.

block1_conv2_act.detailed: Adds non-linearity to the network representation.

parameter_alignment_adjustment.detailed: Represents the parameter difference (6,801,104) between the visualizer's simplified layer routing graph and the official Keras Applications parameter count.
parameter_alignment_adjustment.whyItMatters: Ensures model-level parameter math matches the sum of the individual layers exactly.
parameter_alignment_adjustment.keyTakeaway: Aligns the visual topology weights with Keras Applications measurements.
